w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Functionality testing for login, registration, email verification, and role-based access.
To verify that authentication functions operate correctly for valid and invalid inputs.
This module is critical because it controls access to the system.
2. Enter valid student email and password.
Student user is authenticated successfully and redirected to the student-side interface.
Core access to the system depends on this function
2. Enter valid student email and invalid password. 3. Select sign in.
System rejects the login attempt and displays the message "Wrong email or password."
Register account with valid UNIMAS student email
New account is created successfully and the user proceeds to email verification.
Affects new user onboarding into the system
Register account with invalid email domain
2. Enter non-UNIMAS email and required details.
System rejects the registration attempt and displays the message "Please use your UNIMAS student email."
Failure may allow unauthorised external users to register
Email is verified successfully, user_status is updated to verified, and the user is allowed to continue to the next step without error.
Required before valid user access is granted
3. Attempt to continue using the app.
System prevents access to the main system and displays the message "Your email is not verified yet. We sent another verification email to 90002@siswa.unimas.my."
Failure may allow unverified users to access the system
2. Enter valid admin email and password.
Admin user is authenticated successfully and redirected to the admin dashboard.
Core access for administrative control depends on this function
2. Enter valid admin email and invalid password.
System rejects the login attempt and displays the message "Wrong admin credentials."
Failure may expose admin functions to normal user.
Student accesses system through student login page
System authenticates the student account successfully and opens the student-side interface
Verifies correct access through the student login page
Admin account attempts to sign in from student login page
System denies access and displays the message "
Please use your UNIMAS student email.
Verifies that admin can’t sign in from student login page.
Admin accesses system through admin login page
System authenticates the admin account successfully and opens the admin dashboard
Student account attempts to sign in from admin login page
reroutes back to student login page.
Verifies that student accounts cannot access the admin login flow
Notes: The test data presented in this

are provided as reference values for documentation. The actual data used during system testing may vary, but the field names and overall data structure are consistent with the implemented Firestore database.
Functionality testing for item browsing, searching, filtering, item detail viewing, item creation, and own listing management.
To verify that the marketplace functions operate correctly and support the main listing activities of the system.
This module is critical because it supports the core buying, selling, and donation flow of the platform.
View available listings on home page
System displays the available approved listings on the home page
Search for item using matching keyword
2. Enter a keyword in the search bar.
System displays listings that match the entered keyword.
Search for item using non-matching keyword
2. Enter a keyword that does not match any listing.
System returns no matching listing result
Filter listings using valid selected criteria
System updates and displays listings that match the selected filter criteria.
Filter listings with criteria that produce no result
2. Apply selected filter criteria that do not match any listing.
System returns no matching listing result and displays an empty state appropriately.
View item detail of selected listing
System opens the item detail page and displays the full information of the selected listing
Add new item with complete valid listing information
System stores the new listing successfully and shows a success message
proceeds according to the listing flow.
Add new item with incomplete required information
2. Leave one or more required fields empty.
description = "Used desk lamp in good condition",
System rejects the submission and displays validation feedback for the incomplete required fields.
View own listings in My Item page
System displays the listings posted by the current user.
Remove pending listing from own listing details
1. Open a pending listing from the My Item page.
System removes the pending listing successfully and updates the listing result accordingly.
Notes: The test data presented in this

are provided as reference values for documentation. The actual data used during system testing may vary, but the field names and overall data structure are consistent with the implemented Firestore database.
Functionality testing for buyer-seller communication through the chat system.
To verify that chat functions operate correctly for valid and invalid actions.
This module is important because it supports direct communication before and during the booking process.
Open chat from item detail page for valid listing
System opens or creates the related chat successfully and displays the chat page
View chat list under Buy Item tab
System displays the buyer-side chat list correctly
View chat list under Sell Item tab
System displays the seller-side chat list correctly
Send valid text message in chat
message_text = "Hi, is this item still available?"
System sends the message successfully and displays it in the conversation history.
System does not send the message and keeps the conversation unchanged.
1. Open existing chat containing received messages.
System displays the received messages in the correct conversation order.
Open other user profile from chat
2. Select user name or profile section.
System opens the related user profile page successfully.
Display related item information in chat
2. Observe item section in chat page.
System displays the related item information correctly in the chat page.
Preserve chat history after reopening chat page
System retains and displays the previous chat history correctly after reopening the chat page.
Notes: The test data presented in this

are provided as reference values for documentation. The actual data used during system testing may vary, but the field names and overall data structure are consistent with the implemented Firestore database.
Functionality testing for booking request, booking response, appointment setting, booking details, and booking status flow
To verify that the booking functions operate correctly for valid and invalid actions
This module is critical because it manages the transaction coordination process between buyer and seller
end booking request for available listing
System sends the booking request successfully and displays the booking request in the chat conversation.
Send booking request for unavailable listing
2. Select booking request option for unavailable listing.
request flow and prevents the request from being submitted.
The request button is not available to select.
System displays the booking request correctly in the seller-side chat.
Seller accepts booking request and sets appointment
System accepts the booking, records the appointment details, and updates the booking information successfully.
Seller attempts to accept booking without appointment details
3. Leave appointment date or time empty.
System does not complete the booking acceptance and displays validation feedback for the missing appointment details.
System rejects the booking request successfully and updates the booking flow accordingly.
1. Open booking details from accepted booking flow.
System displays the correct booking details, including item information, appointment date, appointment time, and related user details.
1. Open booking details from accepted booking flow.
System displays the correct booking details, including item information, appointment date, appointment time, and related user details.
System marks the booking as completed successfully and updates the booking status.
System marks the booking as cancelled successfully and updates the booking status.
2. Buyer reopens app or remains in app
System displays the booking accepted notification and allows access to the booking details.
2. Buyer reopens app or remains in app.
System displays the booking completed notification and allows access to the booking details.
2. Buyer reopens app or remains in app.
System displays the booking cancelled notification correctly.
Notes: The test data presented in this

are provided as reference values for documentation. The actual data used during system testing may vary, but the field names and overall data structure are consistent with the implemented Firestore database.
Functionality testing for administrative monitoring and management functions
To verify that the main admin functions operate correctly for user management, listing moderation, complaint handling, category management, and report viewing.
This module is critical because it controls the administrative side of the system.
Open admin dashboard after successful login
1. Sign in using valid admin account.
System opens the admin dashboard and displays the main administrative functions
View user list in user management page
1. Open user management page from admin dashboard.
System displays the registered user records correctly.
1. Open user management page. 2. Select a user record
by selecting view button under action column.
System opens the selected user detail page and displays the stored user information
Update user information with valid input
Faculty of Science Computer and Technology
System updates the user information successfully and displays the message "User information updated."
Update user information with invalid or incomplete input
System does not save the invalid update and displays validation feedback message.
System suspends the selected user successfully and displays the message "User suspended successfully."
System reactivates the selected user successfully and displays the message "User reactivated successfully."
Failure to reactivate will cause student not being able to use the system.
1. Open listing management or pending listing page from admin dashboard.
System displays the pending listings correctly.
System updates the listing successfully and displays the message "Item approved successfully."
System rejects the listing successfully and displays the message "Item rejected successfully."
Reject pending listing without required reason
System block confirmation button for reject making the admin can’t reject the item.
System removes the listing successfully and displays the configured success message.
Remove listing without valid removal reason
System block confirmation button for remove making the admin can’t remove the item.
1. Open report list page from admin dashboard.
System updates the complaint successfully and displays the appropriate success message.
Add new category or subcategory with valid input
1. Open category management page. 2. Select add function.
System stores the new category or subcategory successfully and displays a success message.
Add new category or subcategory with incomplete input
System does not save the record and displays validation feedback
1. Open sales report page from admin dashboard.
System opens the sales report page and displays the recorded sales information correctly.
Notes: The test data presented in this

are provided as reference values for documentation. The actual data used during system testing may vary, but the field names and overall data structure are consistent with the implemented Firestore database.
Functionality testing for sustainability information display and related calculation output.
To verify that the sustainability functions operate correctly and display the recorded sustainability information based on completed item reuse activities
This module is important because it represents the sustainability value of the system
1. Open the sustainability page from the student side.
System opens the sustainability page and displays the sustainability record interface
2. Observe the displayed carbon saved value.
System displays the carbon saved information correctly based on the stored sustainability data.
2. Observe the displayed tree equivalent value.
listing_id from Listing that associated with user_id,
carbon_reduction from the Listing that associated with the user_id
System displays the tree equivalent information correctly based on the stored carbon saved value.
2. Observe the emission factor reference section.
System displays the emission factor reference information correctly.
2. Open the sustainability page from the admin side.
System opens the admin sustainability page and displays the sustainability information
Notes: The test data presented in this

are provided as reference values for documentation. The actual data used during system testing may vary, but the field names and overall data structure are consistent with the implemented Firestore database.
